title: Help me out here I need the Essentials
display_title: Help me out here I need the Essentials
wikitext:
[]: Help me out here, I need the Essentials             Quote Post by Analog-Nub » Tue Sep 26, 2006 10:18 am OK so I'm a the process of finally being able to buy everything Neon Genesis Evangelion on DVD. I did some research on the DVDs and holy shit is their a lot of DVDs! It was too overwhelming, for me at least. I didn't know if I should get the Platinum DVD's individually or just get Platinum Thinpak at the cost of losing some booklets or other bonuses from getting them individually. I'm confused about which one of these DVDs I should get . As I said before, its too confusing for me and I'm hoping some of you true EVA fans can help me out. I'm not as a big fan as the rest of you are but I'm a fan none the less. I'm also a bit of a collector, So things like booklets from the individual DVDs of the platinum series or art books What I'm looking for is just not to get the crappy version of anything and to just get the complete eva story. I want the directors cuts, I want the movies, etc. So basically what I'm asking you: What do you think is the Essentials of Eva? I'm obviously serious about this, so any help would be great. P.S. I'm practically new here in the Eva Monkey Forums, if I have broke any rules, annoy, or anything of that nature I apologize. Please be nice. :)

[]:             Quote Post by Kaysow » Tue Sep 26, 2006 10:30 am I got the Platinum Thinpack, feel head over heels in love with the series, and I am still content with it. All other bonuses are highly downloadable, most from this very site. For the movies; I got Manga's bad releases, then the Japanese Renewal one without subtitles. Gonna burn a copy and add some later.

[]:             Quote Post by Faußtin » Tue Sep 26, 2006 10:33 am Welcome! You're not annoying, but maybe you shouldn't use any special font colour. And reading the Rules is also quite helpful. Though I admit I read it after... around 400 posts? Sorry, I can't help you out, since TdK=wInDoWs in my case. PM me if you have an unstoppable lust to find out whadda heck I meant by that.

[]: Major Suckorxs             Quote Post by Analog-Nub » Tue Sep 26, 2006 10:38 am Kaysow wrote: For the movies; I got Manga's bad releases, then the Japanese Renewal one without subtitles. Gonna burn a copy and add some later.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Tue Sep 26, 2006 10:51 am Dollar for dollar, the Platinum Thinpak is the way to go. You can typically find it for considerably less than its full price. I would hold off on buying Death & Rebirth and The End of Evangelion. Manga Entertainment is bringing out a higher quality version of the movies, and you're definitely going to want to wait for it. The old Manga DVDs make me cry.
